member.name: Apsana Begum
member.synopsis: Apsana Begum is the Labour MP for Poplar and Limehouse , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Iran: Armed Conflict
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the answer of 3 June 2026 to Question 3863, what is meant by permissions to utilise UK military bases being considered on a case-by-case basis; and whether each individual strike requires separa
writtenQuestions.answerText: For operational security reasons, we do not offer comment or information relating to foreign nations' military operations, including the processes for providing them with host nation authorisation. All UK support to allies for operational purposes conside...

writtenQuestions.heading: Palestine: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential implications for its policies of the UN Commission of Inquiry on the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including
writtenQuestions.answerText: The impact of the conflict in Gaza on children is a moral outrage, and the allegations in this report that children were deliberately targeted by Israeli forces are deeply alarming. International law is clear that children must always be protected, and th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gyms: Poplar and Limehouse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of the sustainability of independent gyms in Poplar and Limehouse constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of gyms and leisure facilities, which are great spaces for people of all ages to stay fit and healthy, and play an important role within communities.We would encourage independent gyms to engage with Sport England’...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration Controls: Biometrics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department has conducted any assessments of potential racial, disabilities, or other equalities biases in the use of Facial Age Estimation technology in immigration enforcement contexts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Throughout 2026, Facial Age Estimation technology will undergo rigorous testing and subject to further decision making, is expected to first be implemented in 2027 for small boat arrivals at Western Jet Foil, where quick decisions are critical.Extensive t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Military Alliances
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to question 6647 asked on 3 June 2026 (Israel: Military Alliances), whether the December 2020 agreement has been (a) reviewed, (b) modified or (c) amended at any point since its signing.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK-Israel military cooperation agreement, which was signed in December 2020, remains extant.

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many offences were handled over the course of the 2026 local elections by the national police unit established for protecting people standing for public office.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Operation Ford is the policing support for locally elected representatives, including Metropolitan Mayors, Police and Crime Commissioners (PCCs) and Local Councillors.Working with the police, the Home Office has established the Operation Ford Threat Asses...

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Sanctions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, if she will take steps to bring forward legislation to create a sanctions regime specific to Israel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the statement made to the House by the Foreign Secretary on 9 June, and her answers to the questions raised in response.

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the statement made to the House by the Foreign Secretary on 9 June, and her answers to the questions raised in response.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK-Israel military cooperation agreement, which was signed in December 2020, remains extant.

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the Oral Statement on 27 April 2026 providing an update on the response to the Humble Address. The Government is working to ensure that Parliament's instruction is met with the urgency and transparency that it deserves. As the C...

writtenQuestions.heading: First Aid: Secondary Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has considered including medical first response to drug misuse as part of mandatory first-aid training in secondary schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All state funded schools are required to teach first aid as part of the statutory health education set out in the relationships, sex and health education (RSHE) statutory guidance. Independent schools are required to cover health education as part of thei...

writtenQuestions.heading: First Aid: Secondary Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has considered including first responses to road traffic accidents as part of mandatory training for secondary school education.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All state funded schools are required to teach first aid as part of the statutory health education set out in the relationships, sex and health education (RSHE) statutory guidance. Independent schools are required to cover health education as part of thei...

writtenQuestions.heading: First Aid: Secondary Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has considered including knife wound first response as part of mandatory training for secondary school education under the statutory guidance of common injuries.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All state funded schools are required to teach first aid as part of the statutory health education set out in the relationships, sex and health education (RSHE) statutory guidance. Independent schools are required to cover health education as part of thei...

writtenQuestions.heading: Taxis: Driverless Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has had discussions with trade unions representing workers in the taxicab or private hire vehicle sectors regarding the proposed rollout of driverless vehicles.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Centre for Connected and Autonomous Vehicles (CCAV) has engaged with trade unions as part of its wider stakeholder engagement programme supporting implementation of the Automated Vehicles Act 2024, including through consultation on the Automated Passe...

writtenQuestions.heading: Iran: Armed Conflict
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to his Department's UK MOD Civilian Harm Mitigation and Response (CHMR) Baseline Study 2024/25, published on 28 April 2026, if he will set out the safeguards in place to ensure compliance with inte
writtenQuestions.answerText: Permissions to utilise UK military bases are considered on a case-by-case basis and the decision to grant permission is dependent on the nature and purpose of their activity. This includes consideration of the applicable legal and policy frameworks.

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Nuclear Weapons
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether her Department has engaged Israeli counterparts on the subject of nuclear disarmament and nuclear non-proliferation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 27 April in response to Question 128658.

writtenQuestions.heading: Migrants: Detainees
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department has plans to revise Home Office guidance prohibiting the use of force against a) children and b) pregnant people in immigration removals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office is currently undertaking – until 4 June – a targeted consultation to help inform changes to the family returns process. The consultation seeks views from expert practitioners, children’s commissioners, and key stakeholders on the use of ph...

writtenQuestions.heading: Teachers: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, pursuant to the Answer of 10 March 2026 to Question 117426, Teachers: Pay, what assessment she has made of the impact of differences in pay between further education college lecturers and school teachers on sta
writtenQuestions.answerText: Colleges have the autonomy to set their own teacher pay as it enables them to respond directly to local labour market and skills needs, alongside their own particular specialisation and priorities.In addition, there are pre-existing sector-led arrangement...

writtenQuestions.heading: Teachers: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, with reference to the Answer of 10 March 2026 to Question 117426, Teachers: Pay, what recent assessment she has made of the potential merits of binding collective bargaining arrangements in further education.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Colleges have the autonomy to set their own teacher pay as it enables them to respond directly to local labour market and skills needs, alongside their own particular specialisation and priorities.In addition, there are pre-existing sector-led arrangement...

writtenQuestions.heading: Poultry: Animal Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she has had discussions with animal welfare organisations on the potential merits of prohibiting the import of eggs produced using caged systems for laying hens.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has had wide ranging discussions with animal welfare organisations and other stakeholders on laying hen cage reform, including conversations relating to egg imports. As set out in the UK’s trade strategy, the Government will always consider...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
